title: Warren v0.1.0: manual run path end-to-end
description:
Ship Warren v0.1.0 — the manual run path end-to-end. User can: connect canopy, add project, spawn run, watch live event tail, steer, cancel, see expertise capture round-trip. Single-user, single-host, docker compose deploy.

Out of scope (V2): scheduler, GitHub webhooks, library API exports.

Authoritative source: SPEC.md (V1 sections — see §3.1 V1 goals, §4.3 composition flow, §11.B resolved decisions).

Stack locked: Bun + TS strict, Bun.serve, bun:sqlite + Drizzle, React + Vite + shadcn/ui + Tailwind, supervisor as small Bun parent (§10.3), HttpClient from @os-eco/burrow.

Burrow dependency: shipped (commit 7926a0e on 2026-05-08); all 21 routes warren needs are live.

title: Phase 14 — End-to-end acceptance harness (boot real container, dispatch a real claude-code or sapling run against a sample project, verify §3.1 V1 goals + §11.A reap roundtrip + restart-recovery)
description: Originally framed as the release gate for v0.1.0 (§3.1 V1 goals + §11.A reap + restart-recovery). v0.1.0 was instead validated via SPEC §11.E first-run on 2026-05-09 — a manual real-claude-code run that completed end-to-end (state:succeeded, branchPushed:true) and surfaced three burrow-side gaps shipped in the same session: burrow-7b97 (dispatcher wiring), burrow-55e3 (toolchain forwarding via agents:[agent.name]), burrow-0329 (bwrap --uid/--gid). The harness's role is now regression-prevention for the 0.1.x line — automating the §11.E path so future patches don't silently regress it. Burrow-cli floor is now 0.2.3 (resolves the original Risk #1 in pl-9836). New V1 limitation seeds (warren-dcf3 / warren-93ee / warren-fab1 / warren-3c40 / warren-bd69) interact with specific scenarios — see child seeds warren-c37e and warren-392b for caveats. Stale risks in the original plan: #1 (burrow CLI version drift) is resolved by the 0.2.3 pin; #5 (quality gates exclude scripts/acceptance/) is stale per d7a788e — biome already enforces the tree.

title: Scenarios 09+10 — reap roundtrip (per-run .mulch merges into project .mulch with LWW; seeds the agent closed land in project's .seeds; mulch.record.updated/skipped events visible)
description:
Post-0.1.0 caveats from SPEC §11.E first-run validation:

- warren-3c40: reap currently classifies 'queued, never started' as state:failed indistinguishably from a real agent crash (same observable shape: branchPushed:true, errors:[], state:failed). The stub agent must reach 'running' state before warren reaps it. Either set WARREN_STUB_SLEEP_MS generously enough that the dispatcher transitions queued→running before reap fires, or wait for warren-3c40's fix and assert against the post-fix shape (separate 'never_started' vs 'crashed' classification on the run record / reap event).

- warren-dcf3: the reap branch_push step requires git credential helper for github.com, which the container doesn't auto-wire from GITHUB_TOKEN. The harness should either (a) reuse the GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL insteadOf trick the projects scenario already uses, pointing pushes at a local bare repo fixture, or (b) accept a reap_failed event for branch_push and assert only the .mulch merge / .seeds close roundtrip.
closeReason: Added acceptance scenarios 09 (reap mulch LWW roundtrip — exercises mulch.record.added/updated/skipped events across three sequential runs with prompt-driven mulch_id/ts knobs) and 10 (reap seeds-close roundtrip — fresh-id mode='added'). Extended stub agent (scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/agent.sh) with [mulch_id=...], [mulch_ts=...], [seed_id=...], [seed_ts=...] prompt knobs. Cancel-on-running drives reap inline (mx-bade10) since stub-shell has no runtime-terminal state_change. warren-3c40 mitigated; warren-dcf3 tolerated. Scenario 10 covers mode='added' only — refreshProjectClone wipes reap writes to tracked .seeds/issues.jsonl between runs; mode='updated' branch covered by reap.test.ts unit tests.

title: Container mode + scenario 13 quality gates + ACCEPTANCE.md runbook (wire --container compose-based booting; bun test && lint && typecheck green; document --real, fly deploy, UI smoke manual gates; ml learn / sd sync / sd close warren-3ee3)
description:
Post-0.1.0 caveats from SPEC §11.E first-run validation:

- warren-fab1: warren CLI is not on container PATH. Container scenarios that shell 'warren <cmd>' must use 'bun /app/src/cli/main.ts <cmd>' as a workaround until the symlink (or 'bun link' in /app) lands. Document this in ACCEPTANCE.md alongside the workaround.

- warren-93ee: supervisor has no --no-auth knob for burrow. Container mode must set a real BURROW_API_TOKEN (and matching WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN) in the test compose env; loopback-only dev shortcuts won't work until WARREN_BURROW_NO_AUTH / WARREN_BURROW_ARGS lands.

- warren-bd69: runtime image lacks curl. Container-side HTTP probes need 'bun -e fetch(...)' instead of curl.

- Quality gates: biome already enforces scripts/acceptance/ as of d7a788e (the post-0.1.0 lint cleanup that unblocked CI). Harness code must pass 'bun run lint' from day one — the original plan Risk #5 ('quality gates exclude scripts/acceptance/') is stale.

- Release wiring: .github/workflows/release.yml (eb1a7ca) auto-tags and cuts a GitHub release on every VERSION bump. Add to ACCEPTANCE.md: the harness must be green before bumping VERSION, or a broken release ships unattended.

title: Supervisor can't pass --no-auth to burrow serve (no env knob)
description: src/supervisor/main.ts:364 hardcodes `burrow serve --socket <path>` with no way to inject --no-auth or extra args. Result: warren refuses to boot without BURROW_API_TOKEN even on a loopback-only dev box. Add WARREN_BURROW_NO_AUTH=1 (boolean) and/or WARREN_BURROW_ARGS (array) env knobs that resolveCommandFromEnv splices into burrowCmd. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09 — burrow crash-looped with 'BURROW_API_TOKEN is not set' until a token was set in .env.

title: AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS locks SPA out of its own Login.tsx (chicken-and-egg)
description: handlers.ts:506 sets AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS = new Set(['/healthz']). Hitting GET / with no Authorization header returns 401, but the SPA in src/ui/src/pages/Login.tsx is the *only* way for a user to enter the bearer token. Net effect: warren's UI is unreachable from a browser without the token already in scope. Fix: exempt /, /index.html, /assets/<hash>, and any SPA deep-link routes from the auth check (or move the auth gate to wrap only API routes, not the UI handler). Switch the data structure from Set<string> to a predicate so prefix matching is cheap. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: warren CLI not on PATH inside the container
description: Dockerfile installs four os-eco CLIs globally via 'bun install -g' but does not put warren itself on PATH. Result: 'docker compose exec warren warren register-agent ...' fails with 'executable file not found in PATH'. Workaround is 'docker compose exec warren bun /app/src/cli/main.ts <subcmd>'. Fix: either symlink ./src/cli/main.ts into /usr/local/bin/warren during the runtime stage, or run 'bun link' in /app so the package.json bin entries resolve. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: First-class local canopy support in docker-compose.yml
description: Smoke-testing warren without a real GitHub canopy repo requires manually adding a bind mount to docker-compose.yml so 'CANOPY_REPO_URL=file:///canopy-source' resolves. Lift that into the canonical compose file behind a CANOPY_SOURCE_DIR env var (already wired locally) and document it in README + .env.example. Lowers the bar for first-run testing and avoids requiring a public repo for a smoke test. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: Reap classifies 'queued, never started' as 'failed' indistinguishably from real crashes
description: Warren's spawn flow dispatches a run to burrow, then reaps it immediately. If the agent prompt is under-specified (no runtime/agent section in canopy frontmatter, empty burrow_config), burrow accepts the dispatch but never transitions the run from 'queued' to 'running'. Warren's reap then marks the warren-side run as state:failed even though branchPushed:true and errors:[] — the same observable shape as a real agent crash. Distinguish: (a) burrow run never started (config/runtime issue), (b) started and crashed (agent fault), (c) started and timed out. Surface this in the reap event and run record. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09 (run_ew47b7fzrj25).
closeReason: Added runs.failure_reason column (never_started|crashed|timed_out). Reap infers from warren state on entry: queued⇒never_started, running⇒crashed; overrideable for future deadline reapers. Surfaced on run row, reap.completed event, and RunDetail UI.

title: GITHUB_TOKEN not auto-wired into git credentials inside the container
description: The reap step runs 'git push' for the burrow branch but the container has no credential helper configured for github.com. GITHUB_TOKEN is in env_passthrough territory but never lands in git's credential store. Workaround: 'docker compose exec warren git config --global url.https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/.insteadOf https://github.com/' — non-durable across recreates. Fix: have the supervisor (or a small entrypoint shim) install the insteadOf rule from GITHUB_TOKEN at startup, before spawning burrow + warren. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09 — first run failed branch_push, second run with manual config succeeded (branchPushed:true).

title: Add curl (or wget) to the runtime image for diagnostics
description: Debugging the burrow socket required bun -e fetch() workarounds because the runtime image has no curl/wget. ~1MB cost, saves hours on first-run diagnostics. Add 'curl' to the apt-get install line in the runtime stage of Dockerfile. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: burrow serve has no run executor — warren runs queued forever
description: Warren's spawn flow dispatches a run via POST /burrows/:id/runs (burrow's HTTP API), which calls repos.runs.enqueue() — a DB insert with state=queued. NOTHING in burrow's startServer() instantiates a RunLoop or any executor. Runs sit in the DB indefinitely. Confirmed by reading: src/server/server.ts (no RunLoop instantiation), src/cli/commands/prompt.ts comment 'we deliberately bypass RunLoop — that machinery is for daemonized callers driving N burrows in parallel' (so prompt-the-CLI is the only inline driver), src/cli/main.ts comment 'V1 daemon-less'. Demonstrated empirically on 2026-05-09: /runs/run_myajgag76evw stayed at state:queued, startedAt:null after warren dispatch; running burrow prompt CLI directly with --agent claude-code created a fresh run that DID execute inline (started, failed at bwrap exec — separate toolchain issue). Fix: either (a) startServer instantiates a RunLoop and the warren container runs the executor in the same process, (b) warren spawns 'burrow prompt <id>' as a child process per dispatch, or (c) warren embeds RunLoop using burrow's exported class. (a) is the cleanest. This is a P0 blocker for warren V1 — without it warren cannot execute any agent run.
closeReason: Moved to burrow-7b97 — the fix lives in burrow's startServer (instantiate RunLoop) or its CLI surface, not in warren. Tracking continues there.

title: Toolchain auto-detection misses /usr/local/bin/claude in the runtime image
description: Burrow's collectToolchainPaths returns [] for a burrow whose canopy agent is named 'claude-code', even though /usr/local/bin/claude exists in the warren container. Result: bwrap sandbox spawns 'claude' and gets 'execvp claude: No such file or directory'. Demonstrated by burrow prompt bur_71v3sjxe31x3 --agent claude-code on 2026-05-09 — first event was stderr 'bwrap: execvp claude: No such file or directory', run failed with exitCode:1 in 486ms. Workarounds to investigate: (a) declare [[agents]] in burrow.toml so collectToolchainPaths walks the agent's expected bin paths, (b) extend symlink walker to inspect /usr/local/bin (bun install -g symlinks land there). Belongs in burrow but blocks warren's claude-code agent path.
closeReason: Moved to burrow-55e3. Body itself noted 'Belongs in burrow but blocks warren's claude-code agent path.' — collectToolchainPaths is burrow code; HttpBurrowUpInput change is burrow surface; warren-side wire-through is a thin follow-up. Tracking in burrow's seeds keeps ownership with the code.

title: Run status stuck on 'queued' in UI while agent is actively working
description:
While dogfooding warren on warren-9f65, the UI run view displayed status 'queued' even though the agent was actively running (events streaming, work happening).

Could be either:
- UI bug: misreads/maps the run status from /runs/:id (e.g., not advancing past 'queued' when burrow reports 'running')
- Backend bug: warren never transitions runs.status from 'queued' → 'running' when burrow's run-state reaches running, so the API returns stale state
- Burrow reporting bug: burrow doesn't emit a state-change event warren listens for

Repro:
1. docker compose up -d --build
2. Log in via UI (WARREN_AUTH_TOKEN)
3. Refresh agents, add warren as a project, create a run targeting warren-9f65
4. Observe: run streams events, but the status badge stays 'queued'

Investigation hints:
- Check src/api/runs.ts (status field source) vs src/db schema for runs.status transitions
- Check supervisor/burrow event handler for run.state.* events
- UI: src/ui/src/pages (run detail) — confirm it isn't pinned to initial status from list view

title: Run event-stream bridge dies on Bun.serve 10s idleTimeout, never reconnects → UI looks 'stuck'
description:
Warren's bridge that tails burrow's run events and forwards them to clients on /runs/:id/events drops the connection with ECONNRESET after ~10s of quiet, then ends and never recovers. Run continues in burrow but the UI sees no further events and no terminal status — appears stuck.

Logs (run bur_69zfr73rg82b, 2026-05-09):
  [Bun.serve]: request timed out after 10 seconds. Pass `idleTimeout` to configure.
  [Bun.serve]: request timed out after 10 seconds. Pass `idleTimeout` to configure.
  err='burrow unreachable at unix:/var/run/burrow.sock (ECONNRESET)' written=20 msg='run stream bridge errored'
  msg='run stream bridge ended'
  ... agent kept running in burrow, finalized 'succeeded' ~3min later. UI never knew.

Root causes (one or both):
1. warren's server (src/server/main.ts) calls Bun.serve without overriding the default 10s idleTimeout, so long-lived NDJSON SSE-style streams get killed
2. warren's burrow client uses HTTP-over-unix and the underlying Bun fetch hits the same idleTimeout while waiting for the next event chunk

Fixes:
- Set idleTimeout: 0 (or a large value) on warren's Bun.serve for the events route
- Same for the burrow client when consuming /runs/:id/events
- Add reconnect-on-error in the bridge with seq-based resume so a transient drop doesn't lose events

Related: warren-865e (UI shows 'queued' while agent works) — same root cause, since runs.status updates flow through the bridge.

title: UI overflows horizontally — page requires horizontal scrolling at common widths
description:
The warren UI doesn't fit horizontally on the screen. User reports needing to scroll horizontally to view everything.

Likely causes:
- A page or table without min-width: 0 / overflow-x: auto on the right scroll container
- Fixed-width columns that exceed viewport (runs list, projects list, events table)
- Sidebar + main pane summing to wider than viewport on smaller screens
- Missing max-w-* or w-full constraint on a page-level container

Repro:
1. docker compose up -d --build && open http://localhost:8080
2. Log in, navigate to (likely) Runs or Projects page
3. Observe horizontal scrollbar / clipped content

Investigation:
- src/ui/src/App.tsx layout shell
- src/ui/src/pages/* — check for w-screen / fixed widths / table overflow
- Browser devtools: find the element wider than 100vw

title: Run workspace is stale — agents work on outdated commit, missing recent changes from origin
description:
Warren creates a per-run workspace (e.g. /root/.local/share/burrow/projects/warren/workspaces/bur_<id>) but it does NOT reflect the current origin/<default-branch>. Discovered during dogfooding warren-9f65 on 2026-05-09 (run bur_69zfr73rg82b):

- Host warren repo HEAD: f515d54 (origin/main up-to-date)
- Run workspace HEAD: 2150f40 — eight commits behind, missing the entire scripts/acceptance/ harness
- Result: agent tasked with 'add scenario 04 alongside scenarios 01-03' had no scenarios/ directory to work in. Agent exited 0 having done nothing of substance.

Confirmed in code: src/projects/ has clone (on POST /projects) + rm-rf (on DELETE) only. NO fetch/pull/refresh path anywhere. /data/projects/<id>/ is frozen at registration time forever.

User feedback (must-fix framing):
  'I'm not going to create a new project for every PR/issue I work on,
   that defeats the whole purpose.'

Reference implementation: src/registry/clone.ts (canopy) already does this pattern correctly:
  // First refresh: git clone <repoUrl> <localDir>
  // Subsequent refreshes: git fetch && git reset --hard origin/<HEAD>

Same pattern needs to land for projects.

Design proposal:
1. Before each run's workspace build (POST /runs), fetch+reset the project clone to origin/<ref-or-default-branch>:
     git -C /data/projects/<id> fetch origin
     git -C /data/projects/<id> checkout <ref>
     git -C /data/projects/<id> reset --hard origin/<branch>
2. Accept optional 'ref' on POST /runs body so a run can target a specific commit/branch/tag
3. Add POST /projects/:id/refresh for explicit pull without spawning a run
4. UI: surface project's current HEAD sha + 'last fetched' timestamp on the projects page so users can sanity-check before kicking off a run
5. Persist last_fetched_at + last_head_sha on projects table

Repro:
1. Register a project via UI
2. Push new commits to the project's origin/main
3. Create a new run via UI — workspace HEAD will still be the registration-time commit
4. Agent silently does nothing (or worse, modifies stale code)

Acceptance:
- POST /runs against an existing project picks up new commits without DELETE+POST
- Existing acceptance scenario 03 (projects-management) extended to cover the refresh-on-run case
- Optional 'ref' param works with branch names and SHAs
- UI shows current project HEAD + age

title: DELETE /projects/:id fails with FK constraint when runs reference the project
description:
UI's delete-project confirm dialog promises 'Run history for this project is kept', but the schema declares runs.project_id as NOT NULL references(projects.id) with no onDelete clause (src/db/schema.ts:72-74). SQLite has FKs enabled, so deleting any project that has runs fails with 'FOREIGN KEY constraint failed'.

ESCALATION (2026-05-09 dogfood): the failure is not just 'can't delete' — it's a state-corrupting partial delete. deleteProject (src/projects/manage.ts:142-153) does rmrf(localPath) FIRST, then repo.delete(id). When the FK error throws on the second step, the disk is already gone but the projects row remains. Result: invariant from mx-be7a74 ('a projects row exists ⇒ its localPath exists on disk') is violated. Subsequent dispatches against that project fail with 'project clone missing on disk: /data/projects/...'. Two clicks (delete, then run) wedge the project; UI exposes no recovery path. Bumping to P1.

Recommended fix (option A, unchanged):
- runs.project_id nullable + onDelete: 'set null' on the FK; new migration rebuilds the runs table.
- UI renders orphaned runs (project_id null) on Runs list + RunDetail.
- Matches the dialog's existing promise.

Plus fix the operation ordering even after the FK is corrected:
- deleteProject should do repo.delete(id) FIRST inside a transaction, only then rmrf the disk. If the row delete fails (any reason), disk is preserved and the operator can retry. SPEC §11.E first-run notes (mx-be7a74) explicitly chose the current order to maintain a different invariant (no row without disk), but with onDelete: 'set null' that invariant becomes weaker — the row-first order is safer.
- Wrap the row delete + disk rmrf in something with rollback/recovery semantics (transaction over the row, best-effort rmrf with audit-log entry on failure). At minimum, never leave the system in 'row exists, disk gone'.

runs.agent_name → agents.name has the same shape — file a sibling if/when agent-delete is wired.

title: claude-code agent fails 'Not logged in' — no env-pass path for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in warren→burrow seam
description:
Second dogfood run (run_hkkm35bcckc4, 2026-05-09) failed immediately: claude-code launched in the sandbox, init event fired, then 'Not logged in · Please run /login' and exit. The auth path that's supposed to make this work is broken end-to-end:

1. burrow's claude-code runtime forwards host credentials via forwardClaudeHostCredentials (node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/runtime/claude-code.ts:119): macOS Keychain on darwin, ~/.claude/.credentials.json on linux. Inside the warren container the runtime user is the bun process — its ~/.claude is empty, no Keychain access. OAuth path is dead by construction.
2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY fallback would work IF burrow's sandbox forwarded it, but burrow gates env-forwarding on the project's burrow.toml [env] section. Warren has no burrow.toml.
3. The .env.example (lines 30-32) and SPEC §11.D claim 'mount these into the agent runtime via canopy env_passthrough in the agent's burrow.toml', but parseBurrowConfig (src/runs/burrow_config.ts:32) only reads [sandbox].network — env_passthrough is silently ignored. Docs claim a feature that doesn't exist.

Primary fix is burrow-side (filed as burrow-e9e7): default-allow ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (+ OAuth env names) in the built-in claude-code runtime without requiring project burrow.toml [env]. claude-code is a built-in runtime — its env contract should be built-in too.

Warren-side TODO once burrow lands the fix:
- Decide whether to also wire canopy-level env_passthrough through burrows.up (extend HttpBurrowUpInput, parseBurrowConfig). If we don't, delete the .env.example claim + SPEC line so docs match reality.
- Reap should classify 'agent exited 1 with no model response' as a credential/runtime failure_reason, not a generic 'crashed' (related to warren-3c40 — the discriminator added there only distinguishes never_started/crashed/timed_out today).

Why the first dogfood (warren-5h ago) appeared to succeed is unclear — possibly a manually mounted .claude/ or different canopy frontmatter. The clean dogfood path hits this hole.
closeReason: Resolved as docs-only: trimmed .env.example claim that env_passthrough flows via canopy burrow_config (false today), updated SPEC §11.F entry to reflect docs-only resolution. claude-code env contract is now burrow-built-in (burrow-e9e7 / 0.2.6 default-allows ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + OAuth env names). Project-level env_passthrough plumbing deferred to warren-766b until a non-built-in runtime needs it. Also added the no_model_response failure_reason discriminator (warren-5165's secondary TODO): reap now classifies running-on-entry failures with no model-turn output (no kind=text/thinking/tool_use stdout events) as no_model_response instead of generic crashed, which captures the original 'Not logged in' symptom shape from run_hkkm35bcckc4.

title: Run stays in 'running' state after agent emits terminal result event
description:
Second dogfood run (run_hkkm35bcckc4, 2026-05-09) is permanently stuck in state:running even though claude-code exited 14ms after launch. The bridge captured the agent's terminal result event correctly:

- seq:1 system/init
- seq:2 stdout 'Not logged in · Please run /login'
- seq:3 system result {subtype:'result', is_error:true, terminal_reason:'completed', num_turns:1, duration_ms:14}
- seq:4 system cancel.requested {mode:'forwarded', burrowRunId:'run_2qsnyn57kjzz', burrowRunState:'failed'}  ← added after a UI cancel attempt

GET /runs/run_hkkm35bcckc4 still returns state:'running', endedAt:null, failureReason:null. The seq:4 event is the smoking gun: warren's cancel handler synchronously observed burrowRunState:'failed' (i.e., burrow KNOWS the run is terminal) and still didn't transition the warren row. UI's runs list and RunDetail show 'running' indefinitely.

Per mx-fadaa2 ('cancelRun forwards to burrow without transitioning warren state: Reap is the only path that finalizes a non-terminal warren run'), this is by design — but reap clearly isn't running. Either:
1. Reap is not wired to fire on bridge-stream-close OR on a cancel that observed terminal burrow state.
2. Reap is polling but not finding/processing this run.
3. Reap exists only as code, not actually scheduled in this server build.

Recommended fix path (now with stronger signal):
- In cancelRun: when the burrow response carries burrowRunState in {succeeded,failed,cancelled}, trigger reap for that warren run synchronously (or finalize inline using the burrow state). The handler already has the answer — no reason to bounce through a poll loop.
- In bridgeRunStream: when an event with the runtime's terminal shape arrives (claude-code: kind=state_change, payload.type=result, payload.terminal_reason set), trigger reap for that run synchronously.
- These two short-circuits eliminate the dependency on a separate reap scheduler for the common cases (agent exits cleanly OR operator cancels a stuck run).

UX impact: dogfooding looks broken — a failed run looks like a hung run AND clicking cancel looks like nothing happened. Compounds the warren-5165 / burrow-e9e7 issue: even after burrow lands the credential fix, any future agent crash will look 'running' forever and cancel won't recover it.

Diagnostic IDs: bur_b2h3915n17mk, burrow run run_2qsnyn57kjzz (burrow already has it as 'failed'). Sibling UI bug filed separately for the badge not reacting to incoming events.

title: RunDetail UI doesn't react to incoming events — state badge stale, cancel feels like a no-op
description:
After clicking 'cancel' on a stuck run in the UI (run_hkkm35bcckc4, 2026-05-09), nothing visibly changed: no badge update, no toast, no spinner, no transition. The only feedback was a new event landing in the run's terminal output: seq:4 cancel.requested system {mode:'forwarded', burrowRunId:'run_2qsnyn57kjzz', burrowRunState:'failed'}. The event stream was working — the UI just didn't reflect it.

Two layers of the issue:

1. The state badge on RunDetail (and the row on /runs) reads from a one-shot GET /runs/:id, not from useEventStream. When a terminal/cancel/state-change event arrives, the page doesn't re-fetch. Even after warren-a69a (reap finalization) is fixed, the UI will still show stale state until the user refreshes.

2. POST /runs/:id/cancel returns and the click handler... does what? No optimistic UI, no toast on response, no error if already-terminal. Operator can't tell whether the click registered, whether burrow accepted it, whether warren acknowledged anything. They only see the cancel event in the stream because they happen to be watching it.

Recommended scope:
- useEventStream observers re-trigger a GET /runs/:id when an event with kind in {state_change, cancel.requested, reap.completed} (or any system terminal-shape) arrives. Cheap, simple, covers all the run-finalization paths. Alternative: derive run state directly from the event log, but that's a bigger refactor.
- Cancel button: optimistic disabled state on click, success/error toast on response, surface burrowRunState if returned. If the response indicates burrow is already terminal, immediately re-fetch the run (or, better, the cancel handler finalizes inline per warren-a69a and the re-fetch shows the new state).

Companion to warren-a69a (server-side reap gap). This sd is purely the UI's reactivity to events that DO arrive correctly.

title: os-eco CLIs (sd/ml/cn/sapling/overstory) are dangling symlinks at runtime — installed to /root by build, executed as user 'bun'
description:
Second dogfood retry (run_rpe5tvs06bpg, 2026-05-09) auth path now works (apiKeySource: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in init event — burrow 0.2.6 fix landed cleanly), but the agent immediately tripped on a different DX hole: the os-eco CLIs are dangling symlinks at runtime.

Root cause: Dockerfile's 'bun install -g' (line 48) ran with BUN_INSTALL unset, so packages landed at /root/.bun/install/global/node_modules/. Binaries got symlinked into /usr/local/bin (because the oven/bun:1.2 base image sets BUN_INSTALL_BIN=/usr/local/bin), but the symlink targets pointed back into /root/.bun. Burrow's bwrap profile (burrow src/provider/local/bwrap.ts SYSTEM_RO_MOUNTS) only ro-binds /usr, /etc, /lib, /lib64, /bin, /sbin, /opt — /root is NOT visible inside the sandbox. So inside the agent's UID-1000 namespace, /usr/local/bin/sd resolved but exec failed with 'No such file or directory' because the symlink target literally doesn't exist in the sandbox filesystem.

Fix landed (commit e160401): added 'ENV BUN_INSTALL=/usr/local' before the global install in Dockerfile, and updated the claude-code postinstall path from /root/.bun/install/global/... to /usr/local/install/global/.... With BUN_INSTALL=/usr/local the package store lives at /usr/local/install/global/node_modules/<pkg>/, which sits under the /usr ro-bind, so the symlinks at /usr/local/bin/{sd,ml,cn,sapling,burrow,claude} resolve cleanly inside the sandbox. Recorded as mulch failure record under build domain.

Verification status (2026-05-09): image rebuilt successfully (warren:local, sha 06388ce6a025). Symlink layout confirmed pre-fix: /usr/local/bin/sd -> ../../../root/.bun/install/global/.... Post-fix verification BLOCKED by warren-b060: migration 0003 crashes the supervisor on container recreate against the existing /data/warren.db (drizzle migration transaction makes 'PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF' a no-op, so DROP TABLE runs trips on events.run_id FK). To complete acceptance: either fix warren-b060 first, or wipe the warren_data volume and verify against a fresh DB.

Acceptance (still pending):
- 'docker compose exec warren ls /usr/local/install/global/node_modules/@os-eco/' lists seeds-cli, mulch-cli, canopy-cli, sapling-cli, burrow-cli.
- A fresh 'warren run claude-code <project> -p "sd ready"' shows real sd output in the run events; same for 'ml prime', 'cn list'.

Original symptoms below for reference.

---

The symlinks point at /root's bun store:
  /usr/local/bin/sd -> ../../../root/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@os-eco/seeds-cli/src/index.ts
  /usr/local/bin/ml -> ../../../root/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@os-eco/mulch-cli/src/cli.ts

Confirmed during the same run that 'cn' (canopy-cli) is also broken: the agent's own self-report (run summary, seq:211) says 'Couldn't run the harness end-to-end here — cn (canopy CLI) isn't on PATH in this sandbox, so the fixture builder fails before any scenario runs.' Affects sapling and overstory too — same install path.

Impact during this dogfood: the agent's prompt explicitly said 'use ml', the agent tried 'ml prime', got 'command not found', and gave up on the mulch+seeds workflow entirely. It produced 102 turns of work without ever priming or recording. Worse: 'cn' being broken means the acceptance harness can't run end-to-end inside the sandbox, so an agent working on warren's own acceptance scenarios can't validate them. Affects every os-eco-CLI-dependent workflow.

Found: 2026-05-09 dogfood retry, run_rpe5tvs06bpg, seq:2-21 (sd/ml symptoms), seq:211 (cn confirmation in the agent's own summary).

title: Burrow worktree gitdir is unreachable from inside the sandbox — agent can't run git inside its own working tree
description:
Second dogfood retry (run_rpe5tvs06bpg, 2026-05-09): the agent did 12m48s of real work and emitted a clean terminal event — but the warren burrow worktree's .git file points to '/data/projects/jayminwest/warren/.git/worktrees/bur_b9r7j5evbcg0', and that path is OUTSIDE the bwrap bind mount. From inside the sandbox at /workspace, the gitdir reference dangles. Agent's own note (seq:211): 'sd sync / ml sync / git push deferred — .git in this worktree points to a missing gitdir, so git commands fail.'

Consequences:
1. Agent can't 'git status' / 'git add' / 'git commit' inside the sandbox. So even when the agent finishes real work, there's no commit in the burrow worktree for warren's reap step to push.
2. Combined with warren-a69a (reap doesn't transition), this means: even if reap were fixed today, branchPushed would still be false because the worktree has nothing to push. Today's run produced zero remote branches despite a successful agent run.
3. Expected by SPEC §11.E ('branchPushed: true' was the first-run success criterion), so this is a regression from the first dogfood — either burrow's worktree layout changed or the bind mount changed between burrow 0.2.3 and 0.2.6. Worth bisecting against the working first run if reproducing matters.

Architecturally this is a burrow issue (burrow owns sandboxing + worktree creation), but it surfaces as a warren-side workflow gap — agents running in warren can't commit, can't push, can't 'sd sync', can't 'ml sync'. The §4.3 composition flow assumes a working git inside the sandbox.

Three plausible fixes (in burrow, or warren↔burrow seam):
- Bind-mount '<project>/.git/worktrees/<burrowId>' into the sandbox alongside '/workspace' so the gitdir dereferences.
- Materialize a real '.git' directory inside the worktree instead of using the .git-file-pointing-at-parent layout. Costs disk on every burrow up but eliminates the cross-mount reference.
- Bind-mount the entire project's '/data/projects/<owner>/<name>/.git' into the sandbox at the same relative location. Simplest, also exposes the parent worktree refs to the agent (might be desirable: 'git log origin/main' should work).

Cross-cuts with warren-a69a (reap not transitioning) and warren-1eaa (sd/ml/cn dangling). Today's open dogfood blockers, in order of priority:
- burrow-e9e7 (FIXED in 0.2.6 — credential plumbing)
- THIS ONE (P1 — agent literally cannot commit)
- warren-a69a (P2 — UI stuck running, reap missing)
- warren-1eaa (P2 — CLIs dangling)
- warren-d9ad (P3 — UI doesn't react to events)

Found: 2026-05-09, run_rpe5tvs06bpg, agent's own summary at seq:211. Burrow id bur_b9r7j5evbcg0 (the one with the broken .git pointer).
closeReason: Architecturally a burrow issue; tracked there as burrow-7a80. Fix lands in burrow's worktree+bwrap composition (bind-mount the host worktree gitdir into the sandbox, or materialize a real .git inside the worktree). Reopen on warren side only if a deps bump or warren-side coordination is needed once burrow ships the fix.

title: Migration 0003 crashes on startup against live db: PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF is a no-op inside drizzle's migration transaction
description:
Surfaced while verifying warren-1eaa locally on 2026-05-09. After rebuilding the image (with the BUN_INSTALL=/usr/local fix) and recreating the warren container against the existing /data/warren.db (which had migrations 0000-0002 applied), the supervisor crashlooped with:

  warren: Failed to run the query '
  DROP TABLE \`runs\`;'
  supervisor: warren exited, tearing down burrow

Root cause:
- src/db/migrations/0003_orphan_runs_on_project_delete.sql uses the canonical SQLite "12-step ALTER" pattern: PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF; CREATE __new_runs; INSERT INTO __new_runs SELECT FROM runs; DROP TABLE runs; ALTER __new_runs RENAME TO runs; PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON. (drizzle-kit auto-generated this from the schema change in 490e0ef.)
- src/db/schema.ts:102 declares events.run_id as a FK to runs.id (created in migration 0000_init.sql line 16). With 738 events rows referencing the 10 live runs in the production volume, the FK is non-trivial.
- src/db/client.ts:48 calls drizzle's bun-sqlite migrator, which (per drizzle-orm@0.36 source) wraps the migration body in BEGIN/COMMIT. SQLite's documented behavior: 'It is not possible to enable or disable foreign key support in the middle of a multi-statement transaction... Attempting to do so does not return an error; it simply has no effect.' So the migration's PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF at line 1 is silently ignored, FKs stay ON, and DROP TABLE runs fails.

Why client.test.ts didn't catch it:
- The :memory: and 'fresh file' tests run all four migrations in one pass against an empty DB. Either DROP TABLE works without any data because SQLite's deferred FK validation has nothing to check, or the test path differs. Either way, the 'partial-state file db' scenario (0000-0002 applied, then 0003 added) is the production upgrade path and isn't covered.

Reproduction:
1. Boot warren on a build that includes only migrations 0000-0002.
2. Dispatch a few runs so events accumulates rows referencing runs.
3. Stop the container.
4. Rebuild on the post-490e0ef code (adds migration 0003).
5. Recreate. Supervisor crashloops on 'DROP TABLE runs'.

Three plausible fixes (in increasing surface area):
A) Hoist the PRAGMA out of the migration: in src/db/client.ts:60-67 (configurePragmas), set 'PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF' before migrate(), then 'PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON' after. The connection-level pragma takes effect outside the transaction, so the DROP succeeds. Add a unit test that simulates the partial-state upgrade by opening a fresh DB with only 0000-0002, inserting data into events, then opening with 0003 added.
B) Use drizzle's 'breakpoints: true' / migration runner mode that runs statements outside transactions. Less surgical, may have other implications.
C) Manually rewrite migration 0003 to not use the rebuild pattern (e.g., add an event trigger that mimics ON DELETE SET NULL semantics). Loses the schema cleanliness drizzle-kit generated.

Recommend option A — minimal change, well-scoped, easy test to add.

Also worth adding: a CI gate that runs migrations against a populated DB snapshot (not just :memory:) so the 'partial-state upgrade' scenario can't slip again. Could be as simple as a fixture DB checked in with a few seed rows in events/runs.

Acceptance:
- A docker compose recreate against an existing /data/warren.db (with events rows) succeeds; warren reaches READY.
- A test in src/db/client.test.ts covers the 'apply migration N+1 with FK-referencing data already present' path.
- Post-migration runs/events row counts match pre-migration counts, with no data loss.

Cross-link: blocked verification of warren-1eaa (os-eco CLIs symlink fix); this bug is independent but bites every upgrade past 0002.

Found: 2026-05-09 verification of warren-1eaa, image build 06388ce6a025.

title: Wire canopy burrow_config env_passthrough through HttpBurrowUpInput when a non-built-in runtime needs it
description:
Deferred follow-up from warren-5165. Today, parseBurrowConfig (src/runs/burrow_config.ts) only reads [sandbox].network and silently ignores env_passthrough. Warren-5165 was resolved docs-only because claude-code's env contract is now built into burrow's runtime (burrow-e9e7 / 0.2.6 default-allows ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + OAuth env names without requiring a per-project burrow.toml [env] block).

The structural gap reopens the moment warren dispatches to a non-built-in agent runtime (e.g. a sapling agent that needs DATABASE_URL, or any third-party agent definition that lists env_passthrough in its canopy burrow_config). At that point, do this:

1. Extend ParsedBurrowConfig with a readonly envPassthrough: readonly string[] field.
2. Teach parseBurrowConfig to read [env] (or whatever section the canopy schema settles on — see canopy SPEC) and parse the array of var names.
3. Extend HttpBurrowUpInput on burrow's side (already accepts agents — look for the prior burrow-55e3 pattern) to accept envPassthrough; verify burrow's resolveSandboxEnv unions runtime + project + warren-supplied entries.
4. Forward through warren's spawnRun: src/runs/spawn.ts builds the burrow.up payload — splice in envPassthrough from the parsed agent burrow_config.
5. Acceptance scenario: a non-claude-code agent definition that declares env_passthrough = ["FOO"] in its burrow_config, with FOO present in warren's process env, observes FOO inside the sandbox.

Cross-link: warren-5165 (this seed's docs-only resolution), burrow-e9e7 (the built-in claude-code env contract that made warren-5165 fixable without this plumbing).

title: Reap pushes without committing — silently ships zero work for agents whose prompt has no commit contract
description:
## Symptom

A `warren run` against a real project finishes with `state: succeeded` and `branchPushed: true`, but **zero of the agent's work is on the remote branch**. The agent did real work in its workspace, never ran `git commit`, then warren's reap step ran `git push` against an unchanged HEAD and called it success.

## Witnessed live: `run_a98cfx1fantf` (2026-05-09, 0.1.2 third-dogfood)

This is no longer hypothetical. Reproduced end-to-end:

- Run `run_a98cfx1fantf` against `jayminwest/warren` with the vanilla `claude-code` agent (`canopy-daf3`), prompt `"Work on sd warren-9f65. Use ml"`.
- Final state: `succeeded`, `branchPushed: true`, duration **6m28s**, **49 turns**, terminal event seq:106 = `result subtype=success is_error=false stop_reason=end_turn`.
- Mid-run steer at seq:87 (`steer.sent` confirmed forwarded by warren bridge) telling the agent to commit and push when done.
- Branch `burrow/bur_r9mjn6da9xc9` landed on GitHub at SHA `0c36a339` — **identical to main's SHA**. `gh compare main...burrow/bur_r9mjn6da9xc9 → ahead_by: 0, behind_by: 0, total_commits: 0, files: []`.
- Operator confirmation bias: the human-in-the-loop saw the branch in `gh` listing and reported "I can see the branch/commit in gh" — but there was **no commit on the branch**, just the branch ref pointing at main's SHA. The lie is convincing enough that even an attentive operator misses it without an explicit `git compare`.
- Steer arrived after the agent had already kicked off `bun install` (seq:86) and acknowledged-implicitly by spawning a `Monitor` (seq:96) to wait for `node_modules/.bin/biome` to appear. Last assistant text (seq:105): *"Install is still running in the background. I'll wait for the monitor."* Then `end_turn`.
- This is also entangled with the "end_turn while waiting on background work" structural seam (separate seed `warren-fead`).

This is the *agent-didn't-commit* silent twin of closed `warren-67cc` (gitdir-blocked) and `warren-a69a` (reap didn't transition). Same `branchPushed: true, no actual diff` user-visible shape; entirely different root cause.

## Root cause

Three compounding gaps:

1. **`src/runs/reap.ts:257-265`** — reap runs `git push origin HEAD:<branch>` against the burrow workspace with no preceding `git status --porcelain` / `git add` / `git commit`. If the agent didn't commit, HEAD is the commit the worktree was created from, and `git push` is a no-op-but-non-error.
2. **`src/runs/reap.ts:223,262`** — `branchPushed` is a boolean that flips to `true` on any successful push, including a push that landed zero new commits. There is no `commitsAhead` field, no `reap.empty_push` event, nothing observable that distinguishes a real-work push from an empty one.
3. **canopy `claude-code` prompt (`canopy-daf3` in `understory/.canopy/prompts.jsonl`)** — `system` is `"You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise."` with no commit/push contract. SPEC §4.3 step 6 says reap "pushes the workspace branch" but does not document that the agent must commit first.

The interaction: a thin agent prompt produces a non-committing agent → reap pushes nothing → `branchPushed: true` lies → operator (human or downstream system) thinks the run succeeded.

## Fix scopes (recommended: B + C + D)

**A — Reap auto-commits dirty worktrees.** *Rejected:* silently ships agent-aborted WIP; muddies authorship.

**B — Reap detects empty push and surfaces it.** After push, run `git rev-list <baseBranch>..HEAD --count` (or capture push stderr). Emit a `reap.empty_push` event (or add a `commitsAhead: number` field on the reap summary). Plumb to the UI so `branchPushed: true, commitsAhead: 0` renders distinctly. **Load-bearing — works regardless of agent prompt, would have caught both the empty witnessed instance and made the successful re-run distinguishable in the UI.**

**C — Bake the commit contract into the canopy `claude-code` prompt.** Append *"When you finish, commit your changes with a clear message — warren handles the push, you do not need to push it yourself."* to `canopy-daf3`'s `system`. Note the wording: "warren handles the push" (NOT "commit and push"). Per `warren-1a09`, agent-side push is structurally broken inside the sandbox; instructing the agent to push wastes turns on doomed retries.

**D — Spec edit (§4.3 step 6).** Document the contract: agent commits, reap pushes, sandboxed agents have no github auth path in V1.

## Open question — RESOLVED 2026-05-09 (`run_agpet4ev6e4a`)

Redrafted prompt: *"Work on sd warren-9f65. Use ml. Commit and push when you're done"*. Outcome:

- Agent committed real work inside the sandbox (seq:159) — burrow-7a80 / 0.2.7 gitdir bind confirmed working end-to-end.
- Branch `burrow/bur_0qgh4pwpvgv0` landed at SHA `15339e61` with `ahead_by: 1, total_commits: 1`. Real diff: 5 files (scenario 04 implementation + acceptance lib changes + mulch + seeds updates).
- **Fix C scope confirmed:** instructing the agent to commit is sufficient to make the smoke-test `claude-code` agent ship its work.
- **The "and push" portion of the prompt was inert and counterproductive** — agent-side push always fails (filed as `warren-1a09`). Warren reap is the actual push mechanism, and it pushed the agent's commit successfully because reap runs host-side with `/root/.gitconfig` visible. Fix C wording must NOT include "push" — *"commit your changes; warren handles the push"*.

**Fix B (observability) is still wanted regardless.** Without it, the previous run's empty-branch shape and this run's real-commit shape are visually identical in any UI surface that only shows `branchPushed: true`. The only thing that distinguishes them is `git compare`. That is the load-bearing gap.

## Acceptance criteria

- A run where the agent emits no commits emits a `reap.empty_push` (or equivalent) event AND has a distinguishable summary field (`commitsAhead: 0` or `branchPushed: false`) — the silent shape becomes observable.
- The `run_a98cfx1fantf` shape — branch SHA equal to base SHA, zero commits ahead — surfaces in the UI as something other than a clean success.
- A vanilla `warren run claude-code <prj> -p "<task that produces a diff>"` with no commit instruction in the user prompt lands a real commit on the remote branch (verifies fix C's wording is sufficient).
- SPEC §4.3 step 6 explicitly states agent commits, reap pushes; sandboxed agents have no github auth path in V1.

## References

- `src/runs/reap.ts:131,149,177,223,257-265` — reap mechanics
- `understory/.canopy/prompts.jsonl` — `canopy-daf3` (claude-code prompt)
- SPEC §4.3 step 6 — current "push the workspace branch" wording
- Pattern in mulch: `mx-runs/branchPushed-requires-both-reap-and-sandbox-git`
- Companions: `warren-fead` (end_turn-with-background-work), `warren-1a09` (sandbox push auth)
- Related closed: `warren-67cc` (gitdir-blocked commit), `burrow-7a80` (gitdir bind, fixed in burrow 0.2.7), `warren-a69a` (reap finalize), `warren-dcf3` (host-side insteadOf install — does not extend into sandbox)
- Witnessed runs: empty: `run_a98cfx1fantf` (`burrow/bur_r9mjn6da9xc9`, ahead_by 0); successful: `run_agpet4ev6e4a` (`burrow/bur_0qgh4pwpvgv0`, ahead_by 1)

## Why now / priority

**P1.** Silently no-ops user work AND defeats operator-eyeball verification (a branch in `gh` is read as success). Without B, we have no signal that distinguishes "agent shipped its work" from "agent did nothing committable" — a critical gap for a system whose value proposition is autonomous agent runs. C and D are smaller surface fixes; B is the load-bearing observability fix and should ship first.

title: Agent terminates end_turn with foreground work in flight — claude-code 'I'll wait for the monitor' pattern
description:
## Symptom

claude-code agent run terminates cleanly (`stop_reason: end_turn`, `is_error: false`) while a long-running command (e.g. `bun install`, a `Monitor` polling loop) is still in flight or has not produced its actionable output. The model's last assistant message is something like *"I'll wait for the install to finish"* but contains no tool call, so claude-code's run loop returns to the operator and — with no follow-up user message — the run ends. The agent's intended next steps (commit, push, finalize) never run.

## Witnessed live: `run_a98cfx1fantf` (2026-05-09)

Compounded with the silent-empty-branch pattern in `warren-f3bb`. Sequence:

- seq:86 — agent ran `bun install 2>&1 | tail -20` (foreground)
- seq:87 — operator steer landed (`steer.sent`): "commit and push when done"
- seq:88–95 — agent acknowledged-implicitly: checked for `node_modules/.bin/biome`, tailed install log
- seq:96 — agent spawned `Monitor` tool: `until [ -x node_modules/.bin/biome ] && [ -x node_modules/.bin/tsc ]; do sleep 1; done`
- seq:103–104 — two `task_updated → completed` events
- seq:105 — final assistant text: *"Install is still running in the background. I'll wait for the monitor."*
- seq:106 — `result subtype=success is_error=false stop_reason=end_turn num_turns=49` — claude-code reported success and exited

The model's natural conversational close ("I'll wait...") with no further tool call is read by claude-code's run loop as a completed turn. There's no operator (human or scheduler) to send the next prompt, so the run terminates — even though the agent self-described as mid-task.

## Root cause hypotheses

This is not obviously a bug in any single component; it's a behavior boundary between three things:

1. **claude-code run loop semantics.** A turn ends when the model emits an assistant message with no tool calls. claude-code does not auto-pump after a `Monitor` returns successfully; it returns to the operator. In a non-warren context (interactive REPL), the user would see "I'll wait" and either say "ok, what now?" or wait themselves. In warren, there's no follow-up message coming.
2. **Agent prompt under-specification.** The `claude-code` canopy prompt (`canopy-daf3`) says nothing about "if a long-running command is in flight, poll until it finishes or fails before declaring done." Without that, the model can plausibly decide "I'll wait" is a complete turn.
3. **Warren reap is too eager.** Reap runs immediately on `result subtype=success` (correct, per `warren-a69a`) but doesn't check whether the agent's stated intent (e.g. "I'll wait" / "I'm not done yet") matches the model's `stop_reason`. Hard to detect heuristically.

## Fix scopes

**A — Agent prompt (canopy `claude-code`).** Add to `system`: *"If a long-running command is in flight (a background bash, a Monitor poll), do not end your turn until it has completed and you have inspected the result. Polling output ≠ done."* Cheap, narrow, fixes the witnessed instance. Does not generalize to other agents/runtimes.

**B — Warren-side detection.** Inspect the final assistant `text` event for "wait", "monitor", "still running", "I'll …" patterns. If found, emit a warning event (`run.suspicious_terminate`) on reap. *Brittle and prompt-language-dependent — probably not worth the heuristic.*

**C — Wait for background tasks before reaping.** When reap fires on a terminal event, check burrow for in-flight subprocesses in the workspace. If anything is still running, defer reap by N seconds or up to a budget. *Speculative — would need burrow-side support and a clear definition of "background work in flight."*

**D — Document the seam, do not fix.** Record this as expertise in mulch (`mx-agents/end_turn-with-background-work`) and treat it as a known agent-author footgun. Best for V1 if the witnessed instance is reproduced rarely.

**Recommended:** A as the immediate fix (resolves the witnessed instance and is a one-line canopy edit), D for the long tail (other agents will hit variants of this). B and C are speculative; defer until the pattern recurs across runtimes.

## Acceptance criteria

- A re-run of `run_a98cfx1fantf`'s prompt against the updated `claude-code` prompt does NOT terminate while a `bun install` Monitor is still polling.
- The agent either (a) waits for `bun install` to actually populate `node_modules` before its turn ends, or (b) issues a commit/push tool call before declaring done.
- A new mulch record under `agents` domain documents the failure mode for future agent authors.

## References

- Witnessed run: `run_a98cfx1fantf`, events seq:86, 96, 105, 106
- Companion seed: `warren-f3bb` (silent empty-branch — same root run, separate gap)
- canopy prompt: `canopy-daf3` in `understory/.canopy/prompts.jsonl`
- claude-code semantics: a model assistant message without tool_use ends the conversational turn; no operator means run exit

## Why now / priority

P2: contributes to silent work-loss but only one observable cause among several (the more direct cause is `warren-f3bb`'s reap-no-commit gap). The user is going to retry with a richer prompt; if that fixes it, scope is "prompting issue" and A+D are sufficient. If it doesn't, the deeper plumbing problem becomes urgent.
closeReason: Fixed via canopy claude-code v4 (Scope A): added Expectations bullet preventing end-of-turn while long-running commands are in flight. Recorded failure mode in warren mulch agents/ domain (Scope D). Scopes B (heuristic detection) and C (burrow-side wait) deferred per seed.

title: Sandboxed agents have no github auth path — agent-side git push always fails inside bwrap
description:
## Symptom

An agent running inside a burrow bwrap sandbox cannot push to github.com. Any `git push` from the agent's workspace fails with:

```
fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': No such device or address
```

The agent has no `~/.gitconfig`, no `~/.git-credentials`, no `GITHUB_TOKEN` in its env, and no credential helper. There is no path inside the sandbox to authenticate with github over HTTPS.

## Witnessed live: `run_agpet4ev6e4a` (2026-05-09, 0.1.2 third-dogfood, prompt-fix iteration)

Reproduced cleanly:

- Run prompt: *"Work on sd warren-9f65. Use ml. Commit and push when you're done"* (the *"and push"* was the trigger).
- Agent committed real work inside the sandbox at seq:159 (`git config user.email/name` then `git commit`). Commit landed locally on the worktree.
- Agent attempted `git push` (seq:161, 163, 177, 181) — all four attempts failed with `fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com'`.
- Agent diagnosed correctly at seq:165–169: `git remote -v` showed `https://github.com/jayminwest/warren`, no `~/.gitconfig`, no `~/.git-credentials`, no `credential.helper` set in either workspace or `/data/projects/jayminwest/warren`.
- Agent eventually gave up retrying, model ended its turn, claude-code reported `success`.
- **Warren reap then pushed the commit successfully** (host-side push, `/root/.gitconfig` visible) — the work landed on the remote at `15339e61` with `ahead_by: 1, total_commits: 1`.

The system "succeeded" because reap is the actual push mechanism. The agent burned ~5 turns on push retries that were architecturally redundant.

## Root cause

Two compounding gaps that meet at the bwrap boundary:

1. **`src/supervisor/git-credentials.ts:65-71`** writes the `insteadOf` rewrite rule to `/root/.gitconfig` (because `HOME=/root` for the supervisor). Burrow's bwrap profile ro-binds `/usr`, `/etc`, `/lib`, `/lib64`, `/bin`, `/sbin`, `/opt` — **not `/root`**. Same architectural pattern as `warren-1eaa` (Bun store at `/root/.bun` invisible inside sandbox); fixed there by relocating to `/usr/local`. The git config has the same problem and was not relocated.
2. **`GITHUB_TOKEN` is not env-passthrough'd into the sandbox.** `.env.example:37` documents this explicitly: *"Project-level env_passthrough through canopy burrow_config is not wired."* For `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` the gap is closed via burrow's built-in `claude-code` runtime allowlist (`burrow-e9e7`); for `GITHUB_TOKEN` there is no built-in. Closed seed `warren-5165` recorded the broader env_passthrough gap and deferred it to `warren-766b`.

Closed `warren-dcf3` resolved the *host-side* warren-reap push path; it did not extend into the sandbox.

## Fix scopes

**A — Canopy `claude-code` prompt fix.** Match `warren-f3bb` fix C exactly: instruct the agent to commit only, and document that warren handles the push. Cheap, narrow, addresses the witnessed instance — agents that don't try to push won't see the failure. Does not enable agents that *legitimately* need to interact with github (e.g. `gh pr create`, fork pushes).

**B — Relocate `git-credentials.ts` write target.** Mirror the `warren-1eaa` fix pattern: write the `insteadOf` rule to a path inside burrow's bwrap ro-bind set. Candidate: `/etc/gitconfig` (system gitconfig). The supervisor would need write permission, and the file would persist across container recreates if not under the data volume. Token exposure note: the rewrite URL embeds the literal token in plaintext (`https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/`), so any agent that reads `/etc/gitconfig` extracts the token — equivalent posture to env-passthrough but harder to audit. Recommend this only if env-passthrough is rejected.

**C — Wire `GITHUB_TOKEN` env_passthrough.** Either via burrow's built-in claude-code allowlist (mirror `burrow-e9e7`'s pattern, sibling burrow seed needed) or via the deferred `warren-766b` project-level `env_passthrough`. Explicit, self-describing, the agent then runs `git config url.https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/.insteadOf https://github.com/` itself — or warren ships a helper script. Tradeoff: every sandboxed agent now sees the token.

**D — Accept; document; do not fix in V1.** Treat agent-side push as a non-feature for V1. Update canopy `claude-code` prompt (fix A) and SPEC §4.3.6 to call out the contract: agent commits, reap pushes, full stop. V2 candidate when a use case requires sandbox-side push (e.g. `gh pr create`, multi-remote, branch-to-fork patterns).

**Recommended:** A + D for V1. B if/when the contract needs to widen (sandbox-side push of any kind). C ties to the broader env_passthrough decision (`warren-766b`) and shouldn't be done piecemeal for GITHUB_TOKEN alone.

## Acceptance criteria

For A + D (recommended V1):

- canopy `claude-code` prompt explicitly says *"commit your changes; warren handles the push"* (or equivalent wording that does NOT include "push").
- A re-run of `run_agpet4ev6e4a`'s prompt against the updated canopy does not include any `git push` tool calls.
- SPEC §4.3 step 6 documents the contract — agent commits, warren reap pushes; sandboxes have no github auth path in V1.

For B (if pursued instead/in addition):

- The supervisor-installed `insteadOf` rule is visible inside the bwrap sandbox at boot (path is in burrow's ro-bind whitelist).
- A run that explicitly attempts `git push` from inside the sandbox succeeds without manual setup.

## References

- Witnessed run: `run_agpet4ev6e4a`, seq:163/178/182 (auth fails), seq:159 (commit succeeded), seq:165–169 (agent self-diagnosis)
- `src/supervisor/git-credentials.ts:11-13,65-71` — installs to `/root/.gitconfig`
- `.env.example:37` — documents env_passthrough-not-wired
- Burrow bwrap ro-bind whitelist: `/Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/burrow/src/provider/local/bwrap.ts` `SYSTEM_RO_MOUNTS` (per CLAUDE.md `warren-1eaa` reference)
- Closed: `warren-dcf3` (supervisor insteadOf install, host-side only), `warren-5165` (env_passthrough docs fix), `warren-1eaa` (parallel /root path-visibility fix for bun store), `burrow-e9e7` (claude-code env allowlist for ANTHROPIC_*), `burrow-7a80` (worktree gitdir bind, made commit-in-sandbox possible)
- Companion: `warren-f3bb` (this run is the same one that validated f3bb's prompt-side fix C)
- Deferred sibling: `warren-766b` (project-level `env_passthrough`)

## Why now / priority

P2: real work landed via reap on the witnessed run, so this isn't blocking V1's manual-run path. The agent burned ~5 turns retrying push, but that's a cost not a correctness failure. Becomes urgent if/when:

- A V2 use case requires sandboxed agents to push (e.g. opening PRs to forks, multi-remote workflows).
- Greenhouse or another orchestrator dispatches runs whose prompts include "push" instructions and we don't want to pay the wasted-turn cost.

For V1, fix A (canopy prompt) is the cheap immediate mitigation; fix D (document the contract in SPEC) is the durable one.
closeReason: Resolved by prior work — fix A + D both in place. A: src/registry/builtins/claude-code.ts:24-26 has 'Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream' + 'Do not run git push yourself'; sapling builtin mirrors. D: SPEC.md:150 §4.3 step 6 has Commit/push contract clause referencing warren-1a09. Tests pass (builtins + spawn). Validated by run_agpet4ev6e4a per mx-81bba7/mx-aebb29. B/C deferred.

title: Make CANOPY_REPO_URL optional — ship default agents inline, drop the 'external library required' contract
description:
## Current state

Warren requires `CANOPY_REPO_URL` to be set. On startup it clones the configured canopy repo into `/data/canopy-repo` (or `WARREN_CANOPY_DIR`), reads every prompt tagged `agent: true`, and uses those as the catalog of dispatchable agents. Without a configured library, `warren doctor` fails (`CANOPY_REPO_URL` check) and `warren run` has no agents to dispatch.

In practice the load-bearing content of a typical warren agent prompt is tiny — the canonical `claude-code` agent in our smoke library (`canopy-daf3`) is literally:

- `system`: *\"You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.\"*
- `burrow_config`: \`[sandbox]\nnetwork = \"open\"\`

That's it. Warren is forcing every operator to set up an external git repo, register a CANOPY_REPO_URL, populate it with prompts, just to dispatch what is essentially a one-line system prompt.

## Why this is a problem

1. **Onboarding friction.** First-time warren install requires \"create or fork a canopy library, set CANOPY_REPO_URL, refresh agents\" before you can run anything. Multi-step yak-shave for a tool whose value prop is \"spawn an agent in a sandbox.\" Compare to burrow which works out of the box with built-in claude-code / sapling / codex runtimes (burrow README §17 [[agents]]).
2. **Forces an artificial scratch repo into existence.** We currently maintain \`understory\` (formerly \`my-canopy\`) as a separate git repo whose only purpose is to host two prompts so warren has *something* to clone. That repo has no other reason to exist — it's a workaround for warren's contract.
3. **Library mechanism is over-engineered for the common case.** Canopy inheritance, schema validation, version pinning are valuable for power users with rich agent libraries — irrelevant for the 80% case of \"just run claude-code against this project.\"
4. **Couples warren to a sibling tool's data layout.** Warren's behavior depends on canopy's git layout, prompt schema, and \`cn render\` shellout. A canopy schema change can break warren mid-deploy.

## Proposed change

Make \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` **optional**, ship default agents **inline** in warren's source, and treat the canopy-library mechanism as a **power-user override** rather than a hard dependency.

### Behavior changes

- Warren ships with built-in agent definitions in \`src/agents/builtins/\` (initially: \`claude-code\`, \`sapling\`). Each is a static TypeScript object matching the rendered-agent shape currently produced by \`cn render\`.
- On startup, the agent registry seeds from built-ins.
- If \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` is set, library agents are loaded *additively* — same-name library agents override built-ins; new names extend the catalog.
- \`warren doctor\` no longer fails on missing \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` — it reports \"no canopy library configured (using built-in agents only)\" as info, not a check failure.
- \`POST /agents/refresh\` becomes a no-op (or 400) when no library is configured — TBD which is friendlier.
- \`.env.example\` marks \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` as optional with a clarifying comment (\"only needed if you want a custom agent library — built-ins cover the common case\").

### Files likely touched

- \`src/agents/builtins/claude-code.ts\` (new) — inline agent definition
- \`src/agents/builtins/sapling.ts\` (new) — same
- \`src/agents/builtins/index.ts\` (new) — export the catalog
- \`src/agents/registry.ts\` — seed built-ins on startup; library is additive
- \`src/server/doctor.ts\` (or wherever doctor lives) — \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` becomes a soft check
- \`src/server/refresh.ts\` (or equivalent) — handle no-library case
- \`.env.example\` — mark optional, update comment
- \`SPEC.md\` §10.2, §11.B — document the new contract
- \`README.md\` — quickstart no longer mentions canopy library setup
- \`docker-compose.yml\` / \`fly.toml\` — \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` no longer required

## Acceptance criteria

- Fresh \`docker compose up\` with only \`WARREN_API_TOKEN\` and \`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\` set boots cleanly; UI shows built-in agents; a run dispatches and completes end-to-end.
- \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` is documented as optional in \`.env.example\`, README, and SPEC.
- \`warren doctor\` distinguishes \"library configured but unhealthy\" (failure) from \"no library configured\" (info).
- Built-in agent definitions live in \`src/agents/builtins/\` as plain TypeScript — not as a \`.canopy/\` bundled into the warren container.
- \`GET /agents\` returns built-ins + library agents as a unified catalog with provenance (\`source: \"builtin\" | \"library\"\`).
- Existing operators with \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\` set: zero behavior change. Same-name library agents still override built-ins.

## References

- Current contract: SPEC.md §10.2 (env vars), §11.B resolved decisions
- Warren README: required \`.env\` table includes \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\`
- Smoke-test library being deprecated: \`understory/\` (formerly \`my-canopy/\`) at https://github.com/jayminwest/understory
- Burrow's analogous built-in-runtimes pattern: burrow README §17 \`[[agents]]\`
- Downstream cleanup once this ships: archive \`jayminwest/understory\` GitHub repo, drop local sibling checkout
- Existing canopy-library registry mulch records: \`mx-92452e\`, \`mx-89606e\`, \`mx-a96e2a\`

## Why now / priority

P2. Not blocking any user today (operators have all set \`CANOPY_REPO_URL\`), but it's a structural papercut that surfaces every time a new operator tries warren and every time the smoke-test library needs maintenance. Cheaper to fix while warren is still 0.1.x and the contract isn't yet hardened by external deployments.

title: Dispatch UI has no branch/ref selector — every manual run uses the project's default_branch
description:
## Symptom
`POST /runs` accepts an optional `ref` (branch name, tag, or SHA) and `spawnRun` plumbs it to `refreshProject` so the project clone is checked out at that ref before the run (warren-1bb6 wired this). But `src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx` only collects `agent`, `project`, and `prompt` — there is no way to dispatch a run against anything other than `project.defaultBranch` from the web UI. To target a feature branch you currently have to `curl POST /runs` directly.

## Surface to change
- `src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx:22-39` — add a `ref` state field, include it in the mutation payload.
- `src/ui/src/api/client.ts` `runsApi.create` — accept an optional `ref: string` and forward to the request body (the refresh client at `client.ts:124-125` already takes `ref`; mirror that shape).
- `src/ui/src/api/types.ts` — extend the create-run input type with `ref?: string`.

## Design notes for V1
- **Free-text input, not a dropdown.** Listing remote branches would require an additional `git ls-remote` call per project on form load. Defer that. A text field labeled "Branch / tag / SHA (optional)" matches the API surface exactly.
- **Default placeholder = the project's `default_branch`.** `projects.list` already returns it, so the form can show `placeholder={selectedProject.defaultBranch}` once a project is picked. Empty submission means "use default" — the server already handles `ref === undefined` correctly.
- Server validation is fine as-is: `refreshProject` rejects empty strings and surfaces git errors as `ProjectUnavailableError` → 503/400.

## Out of scope
- Remote branch enumeration (file as a follow-up if anyone asks).
- Persisting "last used ref" per project.

## Test plan
- Manual: dispatch against a feature branch, watch `refresh.completed`-equivalent events / project HEAD update, confirm the burrow workspace shows the right tree.
- Add a UI test asserting the field's value lands in the request body.

title: Auto-open a PR after reap pushes the agent's branch — config-toggleable, default ON
description:
## What's missing
`reapRun` (`src/runs/reap.ts:257-265`) does the final `git push origin HEAD:<branch>` so the agent's commits land on the project's remote — but stops there. To actually review the work, an operator has to switch to GitHub and open the PR by hand. For a V1 whose whole point is 'agent runs → reviewable change', that last manual hop is the missing piece of the loop.

## Proposal
Add a 4th best-effort sub-step to `reapRun` after `branch_push`: `pr_open`. Same failure-isolation contract as the other steps — errors get recorded as `reap_failed` events and the run still finalizes.

Behavior gated by config:
- `WARREN_AUTO_OPEN_PR` (default `true`). Setting to `false` disables globally.
- Per-project override is **not** in scope for V1 — file a follow-up if needed. Single env knob keeps the surface small.

Skip conditions (no PR opened, but don't fail reap):
- `branchPushed === false` (push failed; nothing to PR).
- `branch === project.defaultBranch` or `branch === null` (push went straight to default branch; PR would be empty).
- `outcome !== 'succeeded'` — debatable. Conservative V1: only auto-open on success. Failed runs may have partial commits the operator wants to inspect, but a draft PR for every crashed agent will be noise. Could be a second flag (`WARREN_AUTO_OPEN_PR_ON_FAILURE`) later.
- A PR for `<owner>:<branch> → <default_branch>` already exists (idempotency for re-runs / restart-recovery sweeps).

## How to actually open the PR
Two options:
1. **`gh pr create`** via shell-out. Already available in the container; auth via `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN` env.
2. **Direct REST call** to `POST /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls`. No new binary dep, finer-grained error handling, easier to mock.

Lean toward (2) — the codebase already prefers injected `fetch`/spawn seams for testability and `gh` would be a new runtime dependency just for this one call. `parseGitHubUrl` already gives us owner/name (`src/projects/url.ts`).

Auth: `GITHUB_TOKEN` is plumbed for git credentials in the supervisor (`src/supervisor/main.ts:403`, warren-dcf3). Same token works for the REST call. Surface a clear error event if it's missing, don't crash reap.

## PR title/body
- Title: first line of the run prompt (truncated), or commit subject if push went through.
- Body: prompt full text + run id + link back to warren UI (`<warren-base-url>/runs/<run_id>`).
- Base: `project.defaultBranch`. Head: the burrow `branch` reap already reads.

## Surface to change
- New module `src/runs/pr.ts` with the REST call + the testable seam.
- `src/runs/reap.ts` — add `pr_open` to `ReapStep`, add `prUrl: string | null` to `ReapRunResult`, fold the call in after the existing `branch_push` block (lines 257-265).
- `runs` table — add nullable `pr_url` column (migration), surface in `RunRow` and the UI's run detail page.
- `reap.completed` event payload — add `prUrl`.
- Config: `src/runs/reap-config.ts` (or fold into existing config) reads `WARREN_AUTO_OPEN_PR`.

## Out of scope
- Per-project override.
- Marking the PR as draft based on outcome.
- Auto-merge, auto-review-request, auto-label.
- Updating an existing PR body when re-running against the same branch.

## Test plan
- Unit: PR module with injected fetch — covers happy path, 422 'PR exists' (treat as success, return existing URL), missing token, network error.
- Reap test: assert `pr_open` runs after `branch_push`, populates `prUrl`, emits on `reap.completed`, and is skipped under each skip condition.
- Acceptance scenario: dispatch a run that touches a file, watch reap, GET the PR via `gh api`, assert it exists and has the right base/head.

## Related
- warren-1a09 (sandbox github auth path) — orthogonal: that's about agent-side `gh`/git inside bwrap; this PR-open runs from warren on the host where the token is already wired.
- warren-f3bb (reap pushes without committing) — mostly orthogonal but related: if push pushes nothing, `pr_open` will skip via the 'no commits' branch (`POST /pulls` returns 422).

title: Acceptance scenario 02 — drop empty-list precondition (built-in agents seed pre-empts it)
description: warren-d3e9 / mx-f52e13 added built-in agent seeding (claude-code, sapling) at server boot. scripts/acceptance/scenarios/02-agents-refresh.ts asserts `before.agents.length === 0` before the first /agents/refresh, which is now always 2. title: Supervisor should fail fast with clear error when BURROW_API_TOKEN/WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN are missing or mismatched
description:
Deploying to Fly without these two secrets caused 10 boot-loop restarts before the machine recovered, and a downstream 'missing Authorization header' 401 from burrow on every dispatch (because WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN was unset).

Repro: deploy warren without setting BURROW_API_TOKEN and WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN. Supervisor logs '[validation_error] BURROW_API_TOKEN is not set' from the burrow child, then 'burrow socket did not appear before timeout', exits 1. After the secret was eventually set, burrow came up healthy but warren still 401'd on every POST /runs because WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN was never set to match.

Desired:
- Supervisor validates both env vars are present (and equal, when both set) before spawning anything; exits with a single clear error pointing at the two var names instead of letting the burrow child crash-loop.
- Bonus: surface a startup log line like 'burrow auth: token configured (sha256:abcd…)' on both sides so a deployer can eyeball that they match.
- Update SPEC.md §10.2 + fly.toml comment block to list BURROW_API_TOKEN and WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN alongside WARREN_API_TOKEN.
