Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Phase along the natural seam: lock the cross-repo design and burrow contract first, then ship the foundational layers (`.warren/defaults.json` schema, `runs` columns, port allocator) in parallel, then the reap-time launch sub-step that…

Run `sd plan show pl-2c59` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Sibling of R-19 (plan pl-2c59) — surfaces the broader PR-template configurability that R-19's `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment is the integration point for.

## Problem

`buildPrContent` in `src/runs/reap.ts:552` hardcodes the PR body shape (title, summary, commits, files-changed, seeds, trailer). Projects can't customize what goes into the PR their warren runs open. R-19 adds a need for a conditional "preview URL" footer that only renders when the project has opted into preview environments — and the cleanest place to land that conditional is a configurable template system, not another hardcoded branch in `buildPrContent`.

## Shape

Make the PR body template-driven from a project-local `.warren/pr-template.md` file (or `.warren/defaults.json` `prTemplate` field for V1, with the move to a standalone markdown file aligned with the `.warren/` reorg follow-up).

Template fragments named, no-op when their data isn't present:

- `title` — defaults to the existing 70-char-budgeted title
- `summary` — what the agent reports
- `commits` — list of commits in the branch
- `files_changed` — diff stat
- `seeds` — linked seeds
- `preview_url_or_placeholder` — added by R-19; renders `<!-- warren:preview-placeholder -->`, the live URL, or the failure tail depending on `preview_state`
- `trailer` — Co-Authored-By etc.

Built-in default template ships in-tree. Project override replaces named sections only; unspecified sections fall back to the default. Template language is the same partial/inheritance system canopy uses, so authors don't learn a second syntax.

## Acceptance

- `.warren/pr-template.md` accepted; missing-is-not-error.
- `buildPrContent` consumes template fragments via the named-section system, not string concatenation.
- R-19's `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment integrates as a named section, not a special case.
- Doctor warns on malformed template (unknown fragment names, unclosed tags).
- Documented in DEPLOY + README with an example override.

## Why under pl-2c59 (not a loose seed)

R-19's design lock explicitly defers PR-template configurability here so this plan stays focused. Keeping it under the same plan tracks the dependency: R-19's reap step (warren-f156) ships the `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment as a hardcoded conditional today, this seed pluralizes it later. Order: this seed unblocks the conversion from "hardcoded conditional" → "named fragment in the configurable template."

title: .warren/ YAML reorg — one file per concern + backcompat from defaults.json
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-2c59.

Parent seed: warren-1bcb — R-19 — Per-run preview environments
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Phase along the natural seam: lock the cross-repo design and burrow contract first, then ship the foundational layers (`.warren/defaults.json` schema, `runs` columns, port allocator) in parallel, then the reap-time launch sub-step that…

Run `sd plan show pl-2c59` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Follow-up of R-19 (plan pl-2c59). Cleans up `.warren/` so it stays human-friendly as more knobs accumulate.

## Problem

`.warren/` today is a one-file-per-major-concern mix:

    .warren/
      defaults.json   (global defaults: defaultRole, defaultBranch, defaultProvider, defaultModel)
      triggers.yaml   (scheduler triggers)

R-19 adds a `preview` block. R-15-ish work will add MCP config. The PR-template configurability seed (warren-bd49) wants `pr-template.md`. Continuing to cram everything into `defaults.json` produces a massive, hard-to-diff JSON blob — antithetical to how `.mulch/`, `.canopy/`, `.seeds/` are organized.

## Target layout

    .warren/
      config.yaml          (was defaults.json — global defaults in YAML)
      triggers.yaml        (unchanged)
      preview.yaml         (R-19 preview block lives here, not in config.yaml)
      pr-template.md       (warren-bd49 PR template lives here)

YAML over JSON across the board. One file per concern. Comments allowed (JSON's biggest weakness). Easier code review when a project's warren config changes.

## Backcompat

- `defaults.json` keeps working — loader prefers `config.yaml` when both exist, falls back to `defaults.json` with a deprecation warning surfaced in `warren doctor`.
- `preview.yaml` is loaded if present; otherwise the loader looks for a `preview` block in `config.yaml` (or in legacy `defaults.json`).
- Doc the migration in README + DEPLOY + a `.warren/MIGRATION.md`. No code-side break: existing installs keep running on `defaults.json` until they migrate at leisure.

## Acceptance

- Loader prefers per-concern YAML files; falls back to `config.yaml`; falls back to `defaults.json` with a deprecation warning.
- New installs scaffold with the YAML layout.
- Doctor surfaces the `defaults.json` deprecation warning with a one-shot migration command (`warren config migrate`).
- `.warren/MIGRATION.md` ships with before/after examples.

## Why under pl-2c59

R-19 chose to keep its `preview` block in `defaults.json` for V1 to avoid expanding scope. This seed is the explicit follow-up that hoists `preview` into its own file as part of the broader reorg. Sequenced after R-19 lands and after warren-bd49 (PR-template) since both add files that benefit from the new layout.

Run `sd plan show pl-2c59` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Follow-up of R-19 (plan pl-2c59). Adds a fifth eviction signal — preview teardown on GitHub PR close/merge — once warren has GitHub webhook infrastructure.

## Problem

R-19's design lock (SPEC §11.L) settled on four eviction signals: idle TTL, max lifetime, global LRU, manual teardown. There's a fifth signal that's the *cleanest* one — "the reviewer merged or closed the PR, the preview is no longer needed" — but warren doesn't have GitHub webhook infra yet (R-18 territory). Filing this seed locks in the eventual behavior so when webhook infra lands, this just turns on.

## Shape

- On `pull_request.closed` (merged OR closed-without-merge) webhook for a PR that matches `runs.pr_url`, schedule the preview for teardown.
- Teardown re-uses the manual-teardown route (step warren-d725) so all eviction paths funnel through one idempotent action.
- Emit `preview_evicted` with `reason='pr_closed'` (new reason value alongside `idle_ttl`, `max_lifetime`, `lru`).
- Behaves correctly when the preview is already `torn-down` (no-op) or `failed` (no-op, log).

## Why deferred to V2-of-R-19

R-19 explicitly does not add a GitHub webhook surface; webhooks are a sibling track (~R-18). Building webhooks just for preview teardown would be a wrong-shaped seam. When the webhook surface lands for other reasons (label triggers, issue events), this seed adds one new event handler.

## Why under pl-2c59

Keeps the R-19 lifecycle story in one place rather than scattering eviction signals across plans. Sequenced explicitly after webhook infrastructure (no immediate dependency on R-19 step closure — could even land before R-19 ships if webhook infra arrives first).

title: Widen repo layer to dialect-aware (pg execution path)
description:
Scenario 19 (warren-480a) ships as a skip-gated harness contract because warren's repo layer is still sqlite-bound today. Every repo (agents, projects, runs, events, triggers, workers, burrows) uses drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite's synchronous `.run()` / `.get()` / `.all()` / sync `.transaction` builders (mx-994fa5). drizzle-orm/node-postgres builders are thenable-async (`.execute()` or `await db.insert(...)`); they do not expose `.run()` etc.

To unblock scenario 19 end-to-end on pg:

1. Widen every repo's `db` field from `DrizzleDb` (= `SqliteDrizzleDb`) to a dialect-aware handle. Options:
   - Branch internally on a passed-in `dialect` discriminator.
   - Introduce a thin RepoDb interface and provide sqlite + pg adapters.
2. Replace `.run()` / `.get()` / `.all()` with the dialect-correct call (e.g. `await db.insert(...).values(...)` for both, since drizzle's pg builders are PromiseLike and bun-sqlite builders' chainable result is acceptable via the typed result).
3. Convert sync `.transaction((tx) => {...})` callbacks to async-callback form so pg's signature is satisfied without breaking sqlite.
4. Update ~30 test files to await the changed call sites (mx-994fa5 lists them).
5. Lift the `createReposForDialect` gate at src/server/main.ts:336-344 — that gate explicitly references warren-480a as the unlock; the actual unlock is the repo widening, not the scenario.
6. Run `bun test` with WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres + WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=… to validate the repos against pg through withDb() (mx-73932c).

Acceptance: scenario 19 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-warren-on-postgres.ts) flips from skipped → passed when WARREN_TEST_PG_URL is set against a pg with CREATEDB. Plan pl-f17e acceptance #4 is then satisfied.

The plan pl-f17e bundled this work implicitly into step 7 (warren-480a). Splitting it out so warren-480a stays scoped to the scenario file + harness machinery; this seed owns the repo widening.

title: Complete R-13: widen repo layer to be dialect-polymorphic (pg + sqlite)
description: R-13 (Postgres backend) was announced shipped in v0.3.2 CHANGELOG, but discovery during dogfood deploy (2026-05-14) revealed the repo layer is still sqlite-coupled at the implementation level. Outer wiring (URL parsing, openDatabase, migrations, schema parity) is real, but src/db/repos/*.ts use sqlite-only drizzle APIs: tx.select()...get(), .run(), .all(), sync transactions. WARREN_DB_URL=postgres:// boots, runs migrations, then crash-loops the moment any repo method is called. Two guards (src/server/main.ts:422 createReposForDialect and src/cli/context.ts:64 dialect check) backstop this with a fail-fast error pointing to pl-f17e step 7 (warren-480a) — that's the marker; remove them once repos are widened. Acceptance scenario 19 was skip-gated on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL which CI never sets, so the gap was never caught. See ml record build/postgres-half-shipped-r13 for full context.

title: CHANGELOG correction: v0.3.2 R-13 entry overstates Postgres support
description: The v0.3.2 entry says 'warren now supports Postgres as an opt-in backend via WARREN_DB_URL=postgres://...'. In practice, boot crashes the moment any repo method runs because repos are sqlite-coupled (.get/.run/.all/sync transactions). Either: (a) amend the v0.3.2 entry to call out the gap and point at the follow-up plan (preferred), or (b) add a v0.3.4 entry that explicitly retracts. Also: fly.toml and README mention Supabase + WARREN_DB_URL as a documented path — those need a 'not yet operational' caveat or removal until warren-2680 ships. Depends on: warren-2680 (real fix).

title: Add WARREN_TEST_PG_URL postgres service to CI matrix so scenario 19 actually runs
description: Acceptance scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres) is skip-gated on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL. .github/workflows/ci.yml runs only 'bun test' (no acceptance) — scenario 19 has never actually executed in CI. This is why the half-shipped R-13 (warren-2680) was not caught at merge time. Need: (1) add a postgres:16 service container to ci.yml or a parallel matrix job, (2) set WARREN_TEST_PG_URL and WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres on that job, (3) run acceptance scenarios OR at minimum unit tests in pg mode, (4) ensure the dialect-aware withDb() helper picks up the env var correctly. Depends on warren-2680 (otherwise tests will still hit the guard).
